f: Om Raut's 500 crore Ramayana retelling got roasted for plastic VFX and a chef-viral meme Hanuman dialogue.
c: "Exiled prince chases a ten-headed abductor across a CGI ocean. Monkey army helps. The internet decides the monkeys look worse than the villain."

f: Trivikram's film was pulled from theatres after a French graphic novel's producers called it an uncredited lift.
c: "Heir fakes being a nobody in his own company to find the traitor. Rides a freight elevator. Sacks everyone who smiles wrong."

f: Puri Jagannadh's pan-India MMA debut cast a real boxer as the champion opponent. The film lost reportedly 100 crore.
c: "MMA trainee with a stutter gets into mixed martial arts. Flies to Las Vegas. Fights Mike Tyson. Nobody asks why."

f: Shot over seven years. The Labrador was trained from a puppy for the role. Audiences cried in theatres nationwide.
c: "Factory worker lives alone and hates it. A Labrador breaks in. He tries to evict her. Road trip happens instead."

f: Madhura Sreedhar's NRI dramedy was remade in Tamil. The title spells out the plot before the film starts.
c: "Spoiled Ohio kid gets cut off by his dad. Sent to live with cousins in a Hyderabad lane. Has opinions about the water."

f: V.N. Aditya directed the Prabhas reboot. Not the NTR Sr original. Confusion is part of the fun.
c: "Forest boy turns out to be the lost heir of a faction village. Locals figure it out before he does. Feud resumes next door."

f: Paruchuri Brothers story. Gopichand's second big action lead. Remembered mostly for the stunt choreography.
c: "Medical student moonlights as a masked vigilante. Beats up rowdies between exams. Mom thinks he is at the library."

f: Parasuram's debut. Launched his mass-dramedy template that would define Geetha Govindam years later.
c: "Small-town nobody moves to Hyderabad and walks into a factional war. Picks a side by flipping his late father's coin."

f: Rana Daggubati spent months working with real mahouts. The movie was simultaneously released in three languages.
c: "Lone man lives in a forest with elephants. Developers want the forest. He argues with them. The elephants listen."

f: T. Santhosh's Tamil original Kanithan got a faithful Telugu remake. Nikhil played the journalist.
c: "Freelance reporter finds his degree is fake. So is everyone else's in his batch. The racket goes to the top."

f: Mohana Krishna Indraganti adapted Oscar Wilde's Earnest as a Telugu college comedy. Became a sleeper hit.
c: "Two friends invent fake identities to impress two girls. The girls only like the fake names. Everyone has to keep lying forever."

f: Teja Marni's indie dramedy about small-town boredom. Sree Vishnu plays a graduate with no prospects.
c: "Four jobless graduates stumble into a Rayalaseema smuggling gig. Nobody has a plan. The plan works because nobody has one."

f: Sree Vishnu's dramedy became a sleeper hit on OTT. The title is a Sagara Sangamam song reference.
c: "Middle-class dad promised a rich girl's hand if he finds a better job. Everyone in the family lies harder than him."

f: Palnati Surya Pratap wrote this with Sukumar. Released on Christmas 2022. Nikhil and Anupama Parameswaran lead.
c: "Man finds a stranger's lost diary and falls in love with the handwriting. Spends the film looking for the girl who wrote it."

f: Sampath Nandi's mass masala. Ravi Teja plays a guy who lies about being rich to impress a girl's dad.
c: "Jobless Hyderabad boy fakes a steel-tycoon life to marry up. The girl's dad plays along. The mafia does not."

f: Sriram Adittya's Telugu buddy-comedy had Nagarjuna and Nani leading. Not a Saratchandra adaptation. Hit in 2018.
c: "Pacifist doctor accidentally shares a hospital stay with a Bombay hitman. They become friends. Goons keep interrupting."

f: Mahesh Narayanan shot the Malayalam thriller on iPhones during lockdown. It dubbed in Telugu on OTT.
c: "Whole film happens on laptop screens and phones. A woman disappears after meeting a guy online. Cousin investigates via video call."

f: Arun Matheswaran's pan-Indian period film. Dhanush leads. Telugu dub ran in AP theatres alongside the Tamil.
c: "British-era soldier quits the army, robs a temple, and becomes a bandit. The village cheers. The British are upset."

f: Sudheer Varma's follow-up to Swamy Ra Ra. Naga Chaitanya plays a thief who picks the wrong target.
c: "Pickpocket robs a minister's daughter. Falls for her. Has to pretend he isn't the guy who robbed her."

f: Vamsi directed this low-budget heist. Ravi Teja and Nikhila Vimal locked together in a single flat all night.
c: "Three thieves break into an empty bungalow. The family comes home early. Everyone hides in different rooms."

f: Veeru Potla directed. Manchu Vishnu and Lavanya Tripathi paired up. Remake of Tamil Sundarapandian.
c: "Village rowdy wants to marry a city girl. Her rich family says no. He keeps kidnapping her and apologising after."

f: Karthik Gattamaneni directed. Ravi Teja was cast to look expensive. Pan-India release underperformed.
c: "Cotton farmer in a lonely village is actually a rogue agent. Journalist tries to interview him. Nobody else believes her."

f: Parasuram's follow-up to Sarkaru Vaari Paata. Vijay Deverakonda and Mrunal Thakur lead. Underperformed at the box office.
c: "Architect single-handedly holds up a joint household. The new lodger upstairs is secretly a rich heiress studying him."

f: Vidyadhar Kagita's debut. Vishwak Sen shot at real Himalayan altitudes. Crowdfunded in parts over four years.
c: "Sadhu cannot be touched without dying. Walks to the Himalayas to find a rare herb. Also a mute girl and a devadasi have their own subplots."

f: Radha Mohan's hijack thriller. Nagarjuna played the NSG officer. Shot with real airport cooperation.
c: "Man hijacks a plane at Hyderabad airport with a fake bomb. Real reason is personal. Negotiator figures it out too late."

f: Praveen Sattaru's NIA thriller. Rajasekhar's comeback. Shot across Hyderabad, Portugal, and Georgia.
c: "NIA officer chases a whistleblower across two continents. Villain is a suave IT guy. Everyone runs a lot."

f: Mohan Krishna Indraganti wrote and directed. Nani played a double role. Surabhi and Niveda Thomas headlined separately.
c: "Two women love the same man. One is his fiancee. The other thinks he is dead. He is neither dead nor honest."

f: Ram Gopal Varma directed. Nagarjuna and Sridevi led. Shot partly inside Tirupati with special permission.
c: "Petty thief and a temple runaway get stuck together. They steal something important. Tirupati hills witness everything."

f: Vijay Kumar Konda's follow-up hit. Nithiin paired with Nithya Menen. Title is a Telugu idiom about a racing heart.
c: "Guy walks into a stranger's wedding because the bride looks like his old crush. Turns out she is the crush. Then she sees him."

f: Krish Jagarlamudi's period film starring Pawan Kalyan. Delayed for years over schedule clashes and re-shoots.
c: "17th-century outlaw steals a diamond from a Mughal emperor to fund a rebellion. Sword fights across Deccan forts."

f: Vikram Kumar directed. Akhil Akkineni's second film. Based on a true long-lost-childhood-sweetheart premise.
c: "Boy and girl lose each other at seven. He spends his whole adult life searching. She picks up a random wrong-number call."

f: J.D. Chakravarthy wrote and directed. Shot on a tight budget in the Hyderabad underworld style.
c: "Rowdy takes a supari on a target who turns out to be another rowdy's older brother. Hyderabad old city. Bodies by dawn."

f: Santosh Srinivas directed. Ram Pothineni and Raashi Khanna lead. Built around a corruption revenge arc.
c: "Corporate lawyer takes over his father's campaign against a land-scam official. Dad keeps losing in court. Son keeps escalating at home."

f: Radha Krishna Kumar's debut. Gopichand's solo mass action. Shot mostly in Hyderabad and Visakhapatnam.
c: "Fire department volunteer falls for an engineer whose apartment block keeps catching fire. The builder wants the land underneath."

f: Lokesh Kanagaraj directed. Karthi starred. Telugu dub screened in AP alongside the Tamil. No female lead by design.
c: "Ex-convict picks up his daughter on his release day. Has to drive a truckload of wounded cops through a hostile night first."

f: Maruthi directed. Allu Sirish and Regina Cassandra lead. Built around a slacker-finds-purpose template.
c: "Unemployed friend group blames a runaway girl for everything wrong in their twenties. She returns. Nothing in their lives improves."

f: Krish directed. Rana Daggubati and Nayanthara headlined. Shot across coastal AP's theatre-folk circuits.
c: "Journalist joins a dying village drama troupe to cover a land grab. Ends up acting on stage. Ends up a hero."

f: Hanu Raghavapudi directed. Nithiin and Megha Akash lead. Built around an undercover-agent double-cross.
c: "Intelligence officer poses as a property scam victim. Villain is a guru who runs a fake ashram. Everyone is pretending."

f: V.V. Vinayak directed. Venkatesh led as Lakshmi Narayana. Major box-office hit in Andhra.
c: "Orphaned son becomes a tough factory owner. Fights for his adoptive family's honour against a vengeful ex-employee."

f: Lokesh Kanagaraj directed. Vijay starred. The Telugu dub released day-and-date in Andhra and Telangana.
c: "Quiet cafe owner in the hills gets attacked by gangsters. They think he is someone else. Turns out they are right."

f: Sreenu Vaitla directed. Gopichand and Rakul Preet lead. A family-feud comedy built around two warring groups.
c: "Groom arrives for his own wedding and finds the bride gone. Her family hates his family. He pretends to be a distant cousin instead."

f: Ajay Bhupathi directed. Sharwanand and Siddharth lead. Set in the coastal fishermen belt of Visakhapatnam.
c: "Two best friends grow up in a Vizag slum. Both love the same woman. Both also do crime for the same boss."

f: Maruthi directed. Sharwanand and Mehreen Pirzada lead. The entire plot hangs on the hero's cleanliness OCD.
c: "Hero refuses to touch anything dirty. Is engaged to a girl from a village with buffalo. The honeymoon does not go well."

f: Kamal Haasan's quadruple-role Tamil classic. Simultaneously released in Telugu with same cast. Singeetam Srinivasa Rao directed.
c: "Quadruplets separated at birth. One firefighter. One priest. One corporate heir. One chef. They all end up in the same house."

f: Mahesh Babu P. directed. Naveen Polishetty and Anushka Shetty lead. Opened strong on Independence Day weekend.
c: "Standup comic and a chef both decide they are single forever. Then they agree to co-parent a baby. Now they are dating."

f: Sreenu Vaitla directed. Varun Tej, Lavanya Tripathi, and Hebah Patel lead. The plot hops between Telugu and Spanish cities.
c: "Guy flies to Spain to meet one girlfriend and accidentally meets another. Both girlfriends meet. He is out of explanations."

f: Bhaskar directed. Akhil Akkineni and Pooja Hegde lead. Released during the 2021 reopening rush.
c: "NRI doctor comes home to pick a bride. A standup comic roasts him on stage. He falls for the comic instead."

f: Chandoo Mondeti directed. Naga Chaitanya's left hand was the entire hook. Based on alien hand syndrome.
c: "Man's left hand has a mind of its own. Belongs to his twin who died in the womb. The hand tries to kill the villain without consulting him."

f: Sreenu Vaitla directed. Venkatesh and Trisha paired. Remembered mostly for its Hyderabad-Bangkok mix-up gags.
c: "Unemployed guy prays to a deity, gets mistaken for a gangster, ends up in Bangkok. A real gangster has the same face."

f: Teja directed. Mahesh Babu played a young man caught in a faction war. Broke out Mahesh as a serious actor.
c: "College student watches his father beaten up by a local politician. Drops the books. Picks up the knife. Wins local elections."

f: Gunasekhar directed. Ravi Teja played the cop role in his serious-voice register. Solid but not a blockbuster.
c: "Cop transferred to a new town. First case involves a minister. Second case involves the same minister. So does the third."

f: Hemant Madhukar directed. Anushka Shetty and Madhavan lead. Bilingual Telugu-English on Amazon Prime.
c: "Mute painter witnesses her fiance killed in a haunted Seattle house. Cop investigates. Husband may also be a suspect."

f: Sujeeth directed. Pawan Kalyan plays a retired Bombay gangster forced back into the game. Pan-India release.
c: "Retired don tries to live quietly in Andhra. His old mob calls him back. He arrives wearing sunglasses. Bodies follow."

f: Vijay Kumar Konda's follow-up rom-com. Naga Chaitanya and Pooja Hegde paired. Pooja Hegde's Telugu debut.
c: "Boy falls for a girl in Goa who vanishes home. He follows her to a Rayalaseema village that has never heard of him. Nobody vouches."

f: Shree Karthick directed. Sharwanand leads. The time-travel premise was the first of its kind in Telugu mainstream.
c: "Three friends discover a machine that sends them back in time to 2003. Goal: meet his mother before she died."

f: K. Raghavendra Rao directed. Nagarjuna played Hathiram Baba. Shot on recreated 17th-century Tirumala sets.
c: "Devotee walks barefoot to Tirumala and decides to stay. Falls asleep with his head on the god's foot. The god does not mind."

f: Vijay Kumar Konda directed. Raj Tarun and Malvika Nair lead. Aha's first big direct-to-OTT Telugu rom-com.
c: "Runaway bride hops on the wrong train. The guy next to her lies about his name. They end up at his village wedding."

f: Kodi Ramakrishna directed. Anushka Shetty's breakout fantasy role. Effects-heavy for its year.
c: "Temple dancer is possessed by a deity to stop a black magician. The magician has also possessed half the village."

f: Gopichand Malineni directed. Ram Pothineni and Rakul Preet lead. Built around a fake-family-member scam.
c: "Con man turns up at a stranger's funeral claiming to be the dead man's long-lost brother. Family buys it. He came for the bank safe."

f: Karthik Subbaraj directed. Rajinikanth's return to mass mode. Telugu dub released simultaneously in Andhra.
c: "Hostel warden is suspiciously good at flattening thugs. Old enemies from Madurai arrive at the gate. He is not just a warden."

f: G. Ashok directed. Nani played a spoiled heir forced to live on a village salary. Remake of Kannada Manasaare.
c: "Rich heir told by dead grandpa's will: survive on ten thousand rupees a month for a year or lose everything. He tries."

f: Mani Ratnam's adaptation of the Tamil epic novel. Telugu dub released alongside Tamil. Two-part epic.
c: "Chola prince sails back home to find the empire at war with itself. Everyone is either plotting or in love. Usually both."

f: Mani Ratnam completed the Chola saga in this sequel. Same cast. Telugu dub hit AP screens on day one.
c: "Warring cousins still at it. A dead queen's shadow hangs over every court scene. The throne keeps changing hands."

f: Deva Katta directed. Sharwanand led the Telugu original. Later remade in Hindi with Sanjay Dutt.
c: "Stepson works harder than the real son to inherit the father's political empire. Elections do not wait for families."

f: Damodara directed. Anand Deverakonda played a middle-class groom chasing his runaway new wife.
c: "New groom's wife leaves the house on day three. He does not know if she died, ran away, or just got annoyed. Starts investigating."

f: Radha Krishna Kumar directed. Prabhas and Pooja Hegde lead. Shot partly in Italy with a real train sequence.
c: "Palmist in 1970s Europe predicts deaths for strangers. Meets a woman whose fate line says she will die soon. Panics."

f: Mani Ratnam's Iruvar dubbed into Telugu. Loosely inspired by the MGR-Karunanidhi friendship turned rivalry.
c: "Two best friends become a movie star and a poet. Politics pulls them apart. The stage they started on becomes a battlefield."

f: Harish Shankar directed. NTR Jr and Samantha lead. The title is a nod to a classic Telugu song hook.
c: "College boy falls for a girl whose brother runs the local faction. Whole family says no. He keeps saying yes until a wedding happens."

f: Sudheer Varma directed. Sharwanand led a Spain-and-Hyderabad crime saga. Released alongside Saaho weekend.
c: "Bootlegger flees prohibition-era Hyderabad, settles in Spain, runs a new empire. An old betrayal catches up."

f: Raghava Lawrence directed. Prabhas led this high-body-count action flick. Known for its over-the-top fight scenes.
c: "Son returns home after his father's murder. Finds the killers. Then finds more killers. The killer pool does not stop expanding."

f: Vijayashanti led this female-action vehicle at her peak. Rare hero-style lead role for a female star in that era.
c: "Village woman inherits her father's feud and his grip strength. Thrashes the men her dad couldn't. Marriage proposals follow."

f: Gautham Menon directed. Naga Chaitanya and Manjima Mohan lead. Shot across Kashmir backdrops.
c: "Software engineer meets a girl and loses her the same day. Spends a year and two countries trying to find her again."

f: Sailesh Kolanu directed. Venkatesh played a crane operator with a daughter needing a rare injection.
c: "Ex-gangster lives as a port crane driver with his little daughter. Daughter falls sick. He calls his old friends."

f: K. Raghavendra Rao directed. Venkatesh and Aarti Agarwal paired. Classic family-reunion plot on Sankranti day.
c: "Estranged brothers show up at the family farm for the Sankranti harvest. Old grudges by lunch. New weddings fixed by dinner."

f: Hemanth M. Rao directed a Kannada two-part romance. Dubbed into Telugu. Rakshit Shetty and Rukmini Vasanth lead.
c: "Boy goes abroad to earn enough to marry his girl. Comes home a decade later. She has moved on. He has not."

f: Krish directed. Tarun and Genelia lead. The title references a Mahabharata subplot loose template.
c: "Two Andhra families fix a wedding. The bride and groom have never met. They run away together the night before to avoid it."

f: Sampath Nandi directed. Gopichand and Tamannaah paired. Built around women's kabaddi coaching.
c: "Kabaddi coach agrees to train a women's team. Nobody plays kabaddi in this village. The women learn fast."

f: Satish Vegesna directed. Nithiin and Raashi Khanna lead. Built around a traditional wedding as the climax.
c: "Groom wants a full priest-and-mantra wedding. The bride's modern Hyderabad family refuses. Elders walk out. The wedding happens anyway."

f: Kishore Kumar Pardasany directed. Naga Chaitanya and Sunil lead. Remake of Tamil Vettai.
c: "Older brother is a timid cop. Younger brother is a street brawler. They swap responsibilities to handle a local gang."

f: Atlee directed. Vijay starred. Telugu dub released in Andhra. Known for a long second-half revenge arc.
c: "Small-town bakery owner used to be a cop. A politician murdered his wife. He pretends to be a baker until the politician visits."

f: Vamsee directed. Ravi Teja led a period bandit biopic. Released on Dussehra 2023. Real Stuartpuram setting.
c: "Real-life village bandit robs only the rich in 1970s Andhra. Police are embarrassed. Villagers are grateful. Ends badly."

f: Shiva Nirvana directed. Nani and Ritu Varma lead. Released direct-to-Amazon Prime during the pandemic.
c: "Youngest brother comes home after years abroad. Family land is being sold behind his back. Elder brother is the problem."

f: Mohana Krishna Indraganti directed. Nani and Sudheer Babu lead. Amazon Prime direct release during lockdown.
c: "Cop chases a faceless serial killer who leaves clues at each crime. The killer turns out to want the cop to catch him."

f: Lakshmi Sowjanya directed her debut. Naga Shaurya and Ritu Varma lead. Released Diwali 2021.
c: "Rich girl wants to pick her own husband. Parents keep arranging meetings. She dismisses every candidate. One refuses to leave."

f: Bobby directed. Venkatesh and Naga Chaitanya played uncle and nephew. Winter 2019 release.
c: "Army officer uncle wants his nephew to marry the right girl. Nephew has other plans. The LoC keeps calling him back mid-date."

f: Anup Bhandari directed a Kannada 3D fantasy-thriller. Kichcha Sudeep starred. Telugu dub ran wide in AP.
c: "New inspector arrives at a misty coastal village where children keep disappearing. Locals blame a ghost. He is skeptical."

f: Siva directed. Ajith led the Tamil original. Telugu dub ran in AP. Pongal 2019 release.
c: "Village strongman learns his estranged daughter studies in a Mumbai college. Moves to the city. Acts like a security guard."

f: Ahishor Solomon directed. Nagarjuna played an NIA officer. Produced by Matinee Entertainment. Released Holi 2021.
c: "NIA officer chases a bomber across three countries. Bomber keeps ahead by one city. Team runs out of leads every week."

f: Gautham Menon directed. Nani and Samantha lead. Bilingual with Tamil's Neethaane En Ponvasantham.
c: "Boy and girl meet at fifteen. Split up. Meet in their twenties. Split up again. Meet in their thirties. Voiceover is constant."

f: V.V. Vinayak directed. Prabhas played a ruthless faction hero returning home. One of his mass-era breakout hits.
c: "Construction worker in Scotland discovers he is actually a Rayalaseema heir. Flies home. Faction waits at the airport."

f: Krish Jagarlamudi backed the Krishna Marimuthu debut. Naga Chaitanya led. Dark revenge tone.
c: "Botanist watches his family murdered at a petrol pump. Decides to hunt the shooter himself. The police are not helping."

f: Puri Jagannadh directed. NTR Jr led. Notable flop that inspired a famous meme line: idhi na style ra.
c: "Uncle raises his orphan nephew as a faction bodyguard. Nephew grows up quiet. Waits in the village square. Old enemies arrive at dusk."

f: Deva Katta directed. Naga Chaitanya played an honest garage mechanic squaring off with a real-estate mafia.
c: "Mechanic in a Hyderabad auto-parts district takes on a builder. The builder owns the police, the minister, and the land."

f: Gopichand Malineni directed. Ravi Teja and Shruti Haasan lead. Built around a good-boy-pretending-reckless template.
c: "Rich heir pretends to be a careless party boy so his dad will not marry him off. The dad and the chosen girl both see through it."

f: Srinu Vaitla directed. Ram Charan led. Rakul Preet paired opposite. Built around a mass hero-stunt coordinator.
c: "Hyderabad stunt performer is secretly the backbone of his stepmom's family. Sister is kidnapped. He breaks character."

f: Puri Jagannadh directed. Pawan Kalyan led. Built around a cop-vs-journalist commentary on media corruption.
c: "Honest cop teams up with a bumbling news crew to expose a politician. The lens keeps rolling. The cop keeps swearing."

f: Lawrence directed. Nagarjuna and Anushka Shetty paired. Not a remake, not related to the Amitabh Don.
c: "College professor by day. Underworld boss by night. Students think he is nerdy. Mafia thinks he is scary."

f: Meher Ramesh directed. NTR Jr led. Title misspells country on purpose. Considered a mass-era misstep.
c: "Slum boy grows up to defend his Hyderabad basti from an encroaching builder. The builder's brother turns out to be a sitting minister."

f: Gunasekhar directed. Mahesh Babu led. Followed his first flops after Okkadu. Political-drama register.
c: "Army officer on leave runs for local office to clean up his town. Politicians he used to salute become his enemies."

f: A.R. Murugadoss directed. Mahesh Babu led. Bilingual Telugu-Tamil. Built around phone-surveillance-cop trope.
c: "Intelligence bureau officer listens to phone calls looking for distress. Hears a woman in trouble. The caller is a serial killer."
